page: Microsoft Azure ND96isr_MI300X_v5 is an isolated, GPU-accelerated virtual machine designed for high-end computational workloads. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 8 GPUs and a massive memory capacity of 1850.0 GB, yielding approximately 19.27 GB of RAM per core.

For storage and networking, the server includes 31769 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. It also supports RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) networking, which enables low-latency, high-throughput inter-node communication. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a high-density hardware profile with substantial resource allocation across compute, memory, and local storage. Given its hardware composition, it is engineered for demanding workloads such as large-scale machine learning, deep learning training, massive database hosting, and high-performance computing simulations.
description: Microsoft Azure ND96isr_MI300X_v5 is an isolated, GPU-accelerated virtual machine designed for high-performance computing and massive data processing. Powered by an Intel processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, this server is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 1850.0 GB of system memory, providing 19.27 GB of RAM per core, alongside 8 integrated GPUs. Storage capabilities are extensive, featuring 31769 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support. Additionally, the instance is RDMA-capable, facilitating high-speed, low-latency networking across nodes. This hardware profile is optimized for resource-intensive workloads, including large-scale AI model training, deep learning, complex scientific simulations, and massive database operations.
